mnemonics.title: Widening Order
mnemonics.content: B S I L F D - Bad Students In Large Classes Find Difficult (Byte Short Int Long Float Double)

mnemonics.title: Cast Syntax
mnemonics.content: Target in parentheses before value: (int) 3.99 = 3 - Think of parentheses as 'freezer' that freezes/truncates decimal

mnemonics.title: Up vs Down Casting
mnemonics.content: UP = Umbrella (subclass under superclass umbrella), DOWN = Dive into subclass from superclass

acronyms.expansion: Widening Implicit Conversion Does Increase Numerically (byte→short→int→long→float→double)
acronyms.usage: Remember widening is automatic - no data loss

acronyms.expansion: Narrowing Requires Another Resource Need Explicit cast (needs cast operator)
acronyms.usage: Remember narrowing needs explicit cast - may lose data

visualTips.visualization: Think of number line: small values (left) can easily fit into larger containers (right) - automatic fit
Think of pouring large bucket into small cup - some overflow/loss is expected, need explicit action (cast)
Family tree: child inherits parent traits, so child IS-A parent - upward movement is natural/implicit
Parent pretending to be specific child - risky, need to verify (instanceof) before doing it
Math.floor() cuts off decimal - like chopping off decimal part with knife, not rounding

analogies.analogy: Putting small water bottle into bigger bucket - everything fits, no problem
Putting big suitcase into small locker - need to force/squeeze, some stuff might not fit
Animal→Dog: All dogs are animals, so dog can be treated as animal automatically
Treating generic animal as specific dog - need to confirm it's actually a dog first
Like checking ID before allowing into specific area - verifies identity before granting access
